pi_items.source: Task description, README, verifier.py, and shell scripts all confirm the plan must be saved as EMER.csv
Web search confirmed KSFO is the ICAO code for San Francisco International Airport; verified via AirNav.com

privileged_info_summary: The task is an emergency diversion scenario for a VFR pilot airborne in the San Francisco Bay Area. The origin waypoint is KSFO (San Francisco International Airport). The required plan name is EMER. All 10 acceptable alternate airports are real, FAA-registered airports in the Bay Area: KHAF (Half Moon Bay), KSQL (San Carlos), KPAO (Palo Alto), KLVK (Livermore), KCCR (Buchanan Field, Concord), KWVI (Watsonville), KSJC (San José), KNUQ (Moffett Field), KOAK (Oakland), and KAPC (Napa County). The task uses the real Avare aviation app (com.ds.avare), a free open-source electronic flight bag for Android. The scenario is synthetic, but the airports and task mechanics are based on real aviation procedures and actual Bay Area airport infrastructure.
